_purpose: SEALED grader predictions for the out-of-sample HOLDOUT generalization gate. These fused scores were computed with the grader params FROZEN at commit fef5df6 (provenance anchor) BEFORE any human scored the pairs. The human NEVER sees these values — they are the sealed prediction the BLIND human ledger is compared against. Do NOT copy these into the human_ledger of corpus-manifest.json (that would collapse the holdout into a self-fulfilling fit).
_honesty_note: ALL THREE sealed scores are 0 — the frozen grader's DETERMINISTIC VETO tripped (cap=0) on every pair, firing the CTA / heading / overlap / hero / logo disqualifiers even though all three clones render as visually recognizable, structurally-faithful pages (see .shots/holdout-*-clone.png). This is the headline out-of-sample finding: the frozen veto under-scores good mid/high-band clones to 0. The blind human scores will quantify the disagreement (predicted: human >> grader on all three). This is the gap the holdout gate exists to surface; it must NOT be 'fixed' by leaking these numbers into tuning.

_capture_validity.supabase.com: VALID real holdout (saas). pageH=7694 @1440, coverage 0.72, 99 captured texts. North-star pair.
_capture_validity.posthog.com: INVALID — SKIPPED. Headless/automated Chromium in this network gets a ~900-1000px truncated shell (0 painted-bg, 0 colorGlyph); the multi-section homepage never lazy-expands even with CAPTURE_HEADED true-headed + enhanced settle. Building from a 900px fragment would be an unfair pair. Substituted by linear.app (marketing).
_capture_validity.react.dev/learn: INVALID — SKIPPED. Assets BLOCKED in this network → source renders completely UNSTYLED (raw single-column black-on-white, no CSS/syntax-highlight/sidebar) and ~3.5x too tall (pageH=13753 vs ~3800 styled; coverage 0.14, 143/1045 texts). Documented failure mode (memory: reactdev_source_render_blocked). Substituted by overreacted.io/the-two-reacts (blog).

pairs.visual_read: Real render, not collapsed. All major sections present in correct order (hero 'Build in a weekend / Scale to millions' with green accent, logo strip, 'Trusted by...', 'Start building in seconds', dashboard mockup, 'Join the community', 'Open source from day one', final CTA, footer). Rougher than source: nav has some text-color/contrast issues, some logo cells render as blocks, card grids sparser. Solid mid-band SaaS clone.

Real, strong dark-theme render. Hero 'The product development system for teams and agents', dark editor mockup, all feature sections (self-driving ops, define direction, move work forward, review PRs, understand progress), Changelog cards incl. yellow accent, 'Built for the future / Available today' final CTA. Section order + dark palette match source closely; feature-panel text density a bit lighter than source. Among the better clones.

Real, faithful render — strongest structurally (editorial single-column is the easiest archetype). Title 'The Two Reacts', prose flow, dark syntax-highlighted code blocks with colored tokens, section headings, pink/purple inline-link + button accents all present and in correct order. ~22% taller than source (text reflow blowup). NOTE: distinct article from the DEV-split overreacted 'useEffect' page — no DEV leakage.
